observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Personnel Officer to submit copy of all authority to report to work issued during the period of Enhanced Community Quarantine (March 17- May 15, 2020) and the supporting documents such as Daily Time Record, and accomplishment reports, attendance sheet/logbook for the regular and job order employees to avoid audit suspension.
observations.managementAction: There was no justification nor supporting documents provided to the Audit Team for the hazard pay. The Municipal Mayor and OIC HR assured to secure proper documentation and supporting documents in the future.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to justify the necessity of the specific laptop model purchased, subject to evaluation. Otherwise, such transaction may not be allowed in audit. We also recommended that the Municipal Bids and Award Committee (BAC) justify in writing the excessive price availed for the unit.
observations.managementAction: There was no comment nor justification received from the BAC or Municipal Engineer as the end-user.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Bids and Awards Committee: a) to immediately submit the noted lacking documents for the procurement process and on the evaluation made for the grant of project time extension, otherwise the payments shall be suspended in audit; b) make the demand from the contractor for the refund of the total amount of P296,900.00; and c) require the Municipal Accountant to submit the computations and documents supporting the claim for the noted discrepancies.
observations.managementAction: There was no action taken relative to this matter. The previous and current audit team were not provided the supporting documents for verification.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) concerned contractors to rectify immediately the relative deficiencies noted to conform with the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance Security/Warranty Security and consider blacklisting of the concerned contractors who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support the action taken; and b) Inspectorate Team to conduct regular and strict monitoring in supervision to ensure that all contractors undertake the projects in accordance with the approved program of works.
observations.managementAction: Copies of documents relative to the rectifications made by the contractor on the noted defects were not provided to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to: a) provide the pre-repair inspection report and a written valid justification on the propriety and necessity of the repair, subject to evaluation, otherwise, the said transaction shall be disallowed in audit; b) provide demand from the contractor for the refund of the cost difference plus liquidated damages for the noted delay; and c) submit for evaluation the approved plan for the excavation itemizing the site, dimensions, scope of work, and others. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to draw a correcting journal entry for the understated depreciation of the equipment.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the pre-repair inspection report and justification letter for the repair of bulldozer were not submitted to the audit team as of this reporting. The Municipal Engineer did not initiate the demand for refund from the contractor. The Municipal Accountant did not adjust depreciation.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports and documents of P558,275.00 cash advances, including the supporting documents showing the visited places and activities undertaken for the educational tour, and official acknowledgement receipts for the accommodation and boat hire totalling P45,600.00. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: There was no copies of official receipts and supporting documents for the cash advances for the educational tour submitted to the audit team as of December 31, 2021.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE strengthen the LGU GFPS by completing the composition of the GFPS Execom and TWG to ensure that the LGU GFPS could fulfill its tasks. We also recommended establishing the GAD Database that will strengthen the planning and budgeting processes for GAD and ensure that planned PPAs address identified gender issues, that objectives, performance indicators and targets are well formulated to facilitate monitoring and evaluation for the attainment of GAD objectives.
observations.managementAction: The LGU GFPS composition remains not compliant. Identification of gender issues associated for each of the gender-sensitive activities remain to be based on sex-disaggregated data.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit the missing documents to validate the propriety of the disbursements and ensure compliance with RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: There was no justification received by the Audit Team. The Municipality did not submit the missing documents to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor and Treasurer to refund the amount of P100,000.00 and P9,000.00, respectively and require the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made for P100,000.00 in 2021. The municipal accountant submitted supporting documents for some transactions.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
following:

a. The duly created Inventory Committee, to strictly follow the general guidelines,
procedural guidelines, reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with
property and accounting records, disposition procedures for non-existing/missing
PPEs, procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without
available records of accountability, applicable accounting entries and required
disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements required under COA Circular
No. 2020-006;

b. The Municipal Engineer to provide technical assistance to the Municipal General
Services Officer – Designate in the conduct of physical count of its buildings and
infrastructure assets; and

c. The Municipal Accountant to recognize in the General Fund the noted assets
arising from utilization of Trust Fund and provide for their depreciation since
acquisition. Ensure that succeeding assets acquired under the Trust Fund are all
accounted for in the General Fund.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the
following:

a. The General Services Officer – designate to conduct the physical count of its
Local Road Network and prepare the RPCLRN. Make use of the template provided
in Annex C of COA Circular No. 2015-008 and complete the necessary information
in every field and submit the accomplished RPCLRN to the Accounting Office for
reconciliation with the books. Also prepare the Road Network Property Card for
each Local Road Network;

b. The Municipal Engineer to provide cost segregation of every road networks
owned by the Municipality per road component for the prior and succeeding years
and provide to the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO – Designate.
Likewise, provide technical assistance to the inventory committee in the conduct
of physical count of its Local Road Networks; and

c. The Municipal Accountant to maintain the necessary Road Network Ledger Cards
and have it reconciled with the balance in the Financial Statements. As to the
identified Road Network projects, coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to
provide individual cost per road component to be the basis in the computation of
depreciation expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. record income and expenses in the Trust Fund books upon utilization of funds
received from national agencies or any other sources, whether private or
government, as well as from trust liability for unspent disaster fund from previous
years that are considered as non-exchange transactions; and

b. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance
for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to set
up receivables and deferred income for the RPT and SET every beginning of calendar
year based on the List of Taxpayers certified by the Municipal Treasurer and
recognize income for penalties only for the Municipal share under Tax Revenue -
Fines and Penalties – Property Taxes account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer
reconcile their records to present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-
end in time for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal Accountant;

b. Municipal Accountant to restore to the Cash in Bank and proper liability accounts
the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and

c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during bank
reconciliation such as stale checks;

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider reversion of the payables
outstanding beyond two years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer and
Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Budget Officer to assess
the feasibility of the implementation and responsiveness to the current needs of the
constituents of those unimplemented projects from previous years and revert the
savings from unexpended balances of completed projects. Likewise, ensure optimal
and timely utilization of 20 Percent Development Fund in the succeeding periods by
including development projects that are well-planned and procurement-and-
implementation-ready.

Based on the forgoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the following:

a. the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO)
implement the programs and projects of the Municipality under 70 percent
Mitigation Fund within the intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services
and benefits to the constituents. Likewise, conduct thorough planning and
evaluation to determine programs, projects and activities which are considered
as the utmost priority and necessity for the welfare of the constituents;

b. the Municipal Budget Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation with the Municipal
Accountant and the MDRRM Officer to monitor the sources and utilization of 5
percent LDRRMF; and

c. the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the
Trust Liability–DRRM account per year of transfer as well as for those funds
received from other agencies.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
following Municipal Officials:

a. the General Services Officer to refrain from indicating brands in all purchase
requests forwarded to the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC); and

b. the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to ensure equitable
distribution of welfare goods.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to:

a. require the BAC Secretariat to properly conduct post-qualification of bids such
that no award shall be made to contractors with the deficiencies/inconsistencies
in their bids as noted in the audit;

b. require the contractors to post Performance Bonds with validity until the final
acceptance of the projects and warranty securities commencing from the date of
the final acceptance; and

c. require either retention or surety bond as warranty security.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer issue Certificate of Final
Acceptance one year after completion of projects, notwithstanding the obligation of
the contractor to undertake any defects known within that period.

Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor designate additional
disbursing officers to avoid simultaneous cash advances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to liquidate the cash advances of honorarium, bonuses,
allowances, financial assistance and other similar expenses within five (5) days
after each payday/grant; and

b. Sanggunian Bayan Members and concerned Municipal officials to immediately
settle their unliquidated cash advances. If the cash advances can no longer be
supported with the necessary supporting documents, immediately require the
refund of their cash advances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and
Development Officer to enforce strict implementation of the rules and regulations
incorporated in the amended guidelines of the Municipality in the grant of AICS,
particularly the educational assistance, with the proper category of financial
assistance submitted by the requestor to the social worker and to the accounting
office before processing its payment and liquidation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the submission of
paid DVs with complete supporting documents within the agreed period of
submission. Take necessary actions to provide adequate measures that will enable
the agency to meet the deadlines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require all concerned Department
Heads/employees to submit detailed action plans on the CY 2020 AAR findings and
recommendations, including that of prior years, to be incorporated in the AAPSI, and
to submit the AAPSI to the Audit Team.

Moreover, we recommended that the management follow strictly the above
provisions of the GAA and COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 on the submission of the
AAPSI within 60 days from the receipt of the COA Annual Audit report to give enough
time for the Auditor to review, monitor and evaluate the action taken by the
management on the audit recommendations.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the
Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking documents listed in the
Annex for further evaluation of the transactions.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to
strictly comply with the prescribed documentary requirements before payment is
effected.

In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended and the Management agreed to consider requiring the Municipal Treasurer/Designated General Services Officer (GSO) to create and require an Inventory Committee to dedicate a period of the year, as guided by a the Physical Inventory Plan, on which complete and thorough physical count of the PPEs will be conducted in accordance to the said Circular. After which, require the immediate reconciliation of count results with the accounting records to facilitate the review of the noted variances, effect the necessary adjustments, provision of necessary disclosures in the notes to financial statements, and the necessary disposition for non-existing/missing items.
observations.managementAction: Physical count has not been completed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE. GSO to be able to conduct the physical Inventory Plan and RPCPPE were not prepared thus, the reconciliation between the result of the actual count and the accounting records cannot be made.
observations.reason: The Management was not able to provide manpower support to conduct the physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to: a. record the capital expenditures of P18,270,602.69 as assets and increase government equity thru prior period adjustments in the books of accounts; b. recognize income in the Trust Fund books for any subsequent utilization of funds received from national government agencies or any other sources for CY 2021 for sources that are considered as non-exchange transactions; and c. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted e-copy of JEV No. 101-2021-12-13118 dated December 29, 2021 which pertain to the recording of capital expenditures under the General Fund.
observations.reason: Not Not given due attention by the concerned office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide the necessary disclosures on reconciliation in the Notes to Financial Statements as required in IPSASs 2 and 24 and ensure its prompt submission to the Commission on Audit. The amounts presented in the Reconciliation Statements were immediately coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for revision.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had presented the Reconciliation Statements. The amounts presented in the Statements were tallied with the figures presented in the Statement of Financial Performance, SCBAA and Statement of Cash Flows. Any discrepancies noted were immediately coordinated with the Municipal Accountant for revision.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers, their supporting documents and liquidation reports and documents of the noted cash advances; and b. require liquidation of the P362,000.00 cash advances for the cancelled training.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation report along with the supporting documents for cash advances totaling P339,700.00 were not submitted to the audit team as of this reporting.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the: a. MDRRO to submit copies of work plan for the provision of relief goods to each household and see to it that the date and items being distributed are indicated on the distribution list; b. Municipal Accountant to submit the original copy of sales invoice and collection receipt issued by BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines as well as collection receipt of distribution list for the relief goods, face mask, face shield and school supplies; c. BAC to ensure that the name and address of the entity, and its owner or printed name of the person providing the quote, contact number, email address (if any) are indicated in the Request for Quotation and Requests for Quotation; and d. BAC to submit the following: - approved Annual Procurement Plan or any supplemental or revision thereof inclusive of all documents relative to the purchases made in response to COVID-19 pandemic; - BAC Resolution for the justification of alternative mode of procurement and recommendation for the award of the contract, proof of posting of Request for Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order, ITR for CY 2019, original copy of Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) and Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit for CY 2020 of BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines; and - justification for inconsistencies in the posting of Notice for Negotiated Procurement in the PhilGEPs, Contract Agreement and distribution of the relief goods purchased from L and H Marketing under Check No. 1758900.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman had commented during the exit that future Purchase Requests will not be indicative of brands and will only include it in the Purchase Orders. The Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer took note of the observation and agreed to comply with the recommendation given. Distribution shall always be based on population database.
observations.reason: The identified number of documents and distribution list for verification were not submitted as of this reporting.

observations.recommendation: Therefore, we recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to specify the details of purpose of hiring boats as transportation in order to substantiate the propriety of each transaction.
observations.managementAction: Based on the submitted Purchase Orders of boat hires of CY 2021, the Municipality already included the purpose and the details of travels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the optimal utilization of the 20 Percent Development Fund and require the: a. Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating expenditures that are not allowed to be charged against 20 percent Development Fund and record obligation upon entering into contract for project implementation; b. Municipal Accountant to submit the sales invoice and collection receipt for the purchase of 3 units aircondition from Kendra Aircondition Trading; and c. Former Acting MAO to provide the Audit Team with the detailed plan for the Greening Project and its proper costing and justification on the noted discrepancies between their reported distribution of seedlings with the result of confirmation with the 13 Punong Barangays, as well as the status of the trees planted; d. BAC Chairperson provide copy of Invitation to Bid which posted in the PhilGEPS and provide explanation for the inconsistencies noted on the posted Award Notice Abstract.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had submitted the JEV to the audit team for the erroneous transfer of unutilized capital outlay amounting to P122,885.00. The Municipal Budget Officer noted that for CY 2021, the Municipality had utilized the 20% Development Fund for PPAs which are allowed to be charged under the development fund. The Former Acting MAO did not submit the Detailed Plan for the Greening Project. BAC Chairperson did not provide explanation on the inconsistencies in the posting of Award Notices. The Municipal Accountant submitted Sales Invoice No. 0012 dated August 7, 2020 and Collection Receipt No. 0028 relative to the purchase of the three air conditioning units.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment for erroneous transfer of unutilized capital outlay amounting to P122,885.00 and for the payments made to job order workers totaling P27,115.00; b. prepare Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit same to the Audit Team on or before 15th day after the end of each month; and c. Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that only disaster risk management activities will be charged to the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had submitted the JEV to the audit team for the adjustment on P122,885.00. The Municipal Accountant had assured that subsidiary ledgers for Trust Liability – DRRMF per year of transfer will be prepared. Fund transfers intended for DRRM purposes will also be included in Trust Liability – DRRMF account.
observations.reason: The details for the appropriation and utilization of the 5% LDRRM Fund as of December 31, 2021 were not provided in the Notes to Financial Statements. Likewise, the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF were not submitted to the audit team on the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant should ensure the completeness of the documents authenticating the claims for reimbursement, with specification of the particular purpose of travel, prior to its approval and processing.
observations.managementAction: Traveling claims were supported with sufficient documentation as recommended.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to undertake immediate disposal of unserviceable PPE to declutter the accounting books of non-value items.
observations.managementAction: There was no disposal of properties made in 2021.
observations.reason: The Management was not able to provide manpower support to conduct the physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: • require deposit of the collections intact and at least once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00; and • open a separate bank account intended for the EUF collections only.
observations.managementAction: Due to the onset of pandemic, there is no EUF collection. The Accounting Office maintains a separate subsidiary ledger for the collection to monitor expenses therefrom.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact and regularly all collections. The deposits should be made at the end of each month and any lapping be immediately corrected.
observations.managementAction: Intact deposit of collections was not observed for 2021. It was only at the end of the year that they made various deposits as can be seen in the cash receipts journal for the month of December 2021.
observations.reason: There are still months that they are not making any deposit and holding cash ranging from P36,387.28 to P338,433.46 at the end of the month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to require the refund of the remaining P50,000.00 disbursed without proper budget obligation. Moving forward, require the Municipal Accountant to require all financial assistance granted by the Office of the Mayor be supported with a valid identification of the payee and basis of crisis situation being claimed for.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had ensured submission of the lacking documents to the audit team. Implementation of refunds were initiated: The Sangguniang Bayan Members and other concerned Municipal Officials already initiated the refund for the LLES-5 Seminar. Official Receipt Nos. 29593540 to 29593548 were issued for the refund of P227,000.00. The partial refund was made on May 20 and 23, 2022. The Municipal Treasurer likewise refunded a total of P80,000.00 under Official Receipt Nos. 29593596 dated May 27, 2022 and 29593614 dated May 31, 2022. Remaining balance for refund amounts to P100,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to compute and claim from the contractor the liquidated damages incurred for the three (3) projects. Otherwise, such amount of damages not deducted from the payment to the contractor shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: No projects were noted with delay based on Quarterly Monitoring of Government Projects, Programs and Activities.
observations.reason: For the three (3) projects, claiming the liquidated damages to the contractor despite their payment in CY 2019 was not provided in the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the immediate submission of the noted lacking documents. Otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted some of the documents but some supporting documents were still not provided to the audit team for verification.
observations.reason: The required documents were not completely provided to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and concerned officials to refund the amount of P100,000.00 and P9,000.00, respectively, representing unsubstantiated payments, and to stop hiring laborers and instead incorporate necessary construction/preparatory works in the Program of Works for projects awarded to contractors.
observations.managementAction: No refund was made in 2021. It was noted that no hiring of laborers was done in 2021 during the scope of audit.
observations.reason: There is still balance of P50,000.00 subject for recoupment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the concerned officials/employees to submit the liquidation reports and documents of P114,600.00 cash advances, Itinerary of Travel and Reimbursement Expense Receipt (RER) for van hire totalling P121,743.10. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit. We also recommended the attendees of 2nd Local Legislation Enhancement Seminar (LLES-2) to refund the expenses totalling P90,839.32 that could have been dispensed if the seminar attended was held in Luzon. Likewise, attending seminars, trainings and other related activities within the geographical island is recommended.
observations.managementAction: Out of P95,700.00, a total of P76,980.00 travel expenses had been refunded with a balance of P18,720.00. Full settlement was not made in 2021.
observations.reason: There is still balance of P18,720.00 not refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the missing DVs and their related supporting documents as well as the lacking documents noted, otherwise the transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: The missing DVs and their related supporting documents were eventually submitted; however some related supporting documents remain unsubmitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct all departments that in the preparation of their Purchase Orders, detailed information are to be indicated particularly, the specifications of the items being procured. Also, for the items/goods procured under the listed POs without details, we recommended the submission of the pictures of the items delivered in order to ascertain the type/kind of item procured.
observations.managementAction: The submitted Purchase Orders of the Municipality for CY 2021 were indicative of the required details.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial account and Accountant to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/ Due to LGUs by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Records verification showed discrepancies of P32,539.44 in the Due from LGUs account and P96,937.85 in Due to LGUs account of the Municipality as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended strict compliance with the provisions of the COA Circulars and immediate submission of the missing disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: No subsequent submission of the remaining 2017 DVs were made by the Municipality for CY 2021.
observations.reason: No subsequent submission of the remaining 2017 DVs was made by the Municipality for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (PPA) and those that are to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above-cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: The submitted Report on Government Programs, Projects and Activities for 1st to 4th Quarter of CY 2021 included PPAs which were on-going and for implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that the accountable officers of the Municipality are duly bonded and strictly observe the requirements of Treasury Circular 02-2009 on the bonding of public officers and ensure that the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality is covered by fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: The members of the Municipal Officials were duly bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require the OSCA and the MSWDO to conduct the necessary verification/background check and home visitation in determining the final list of the Social Pensioners and any amendments thereto should be properly certified and approved.
observations.managementAction: The LGU OSCA and MSWDO re-validated the qualifications of the senior citizens receiving pension and conducted verification/home visit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. review the composition of the Solid Waste Management Board and, if necessary, reorganize it in accordance to the requirements provided under Section 12 of RA No. 9003 to ensure effective and efficient formulation and implementation of the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan; b. require the MSWMB to compile the documents pertaining to the Solid Waste Management Plan (SWMP) of the Municipality, verify the status of its approval with the National Solid Waste Management Commission, and regularly convene to enable effective performance of its functions; and c. thru the MSWMB require strict implementation of all Municipal Ordinances and impose penalties to those who will be caught violating the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan has been submitted for review.
observations.reason: Recommended revisions are still being worked out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to coordinate with the source agency itself regarding the return of the remaining balances of the completed programs/projects. Also ensure proper remittance of VAT withheld to BIR and maintain separate subsidiary ledger per program/project.
observations.managementAction: The submitted subsidiary ledgers of the Due to NGAs account still showed balances of completed programs and projects which remained non-moving and had been carried over as beginning balances of CY 2021. Likewise, documents showing the coordination made by the Municipality with source agency were not submitted to the audit team for verification.
observations.reason: The source agency coordination details were not submitted to the audit team for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the members of the BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit justification why the Request for Quotation was used instead of the competitive bidding and to submit the missing documents in order to validate the propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: The previous Municipal Accountant submitted some documents but full justification and supporting documents were not received by the current audit team.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters showing the submission of lacking documents to the previous and current audit team were not provided to the current audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer intensify the monitoring of all ongoing projects to avoid delays and ensure completion in compliance with the approved contract period.
observations.managementAction: No projects were noted unfinished in the scope of audit based on Quarterly Monitoring of Government Projects, Programs and Activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Mayor to prioritize the payments of terminal leave benefits and ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer processed and paid terminal leave benefits and the Municipality is willing to process claims for terminal leave benefits, but some retirees were not able to submit the requirements to claim.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant stated that they are willing to process claims for terminal leave benefits, but some retirees were not able to submit the requirements to be able to claim.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the computations and documents supporting the payments made; and the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit the missing bidding documents and justification.
observations.managementAction: The previous Municipal Accountant submitted some documents but full set of supporting documents and justifications were not provided to the audit team.
observations.reason: Not given due attention by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended No action taken Implemented Moreover, recommended to undertake the disposal of unserviceable PPE to declutter the accounting books of non-value items.
observations.managementAction: The Management is deliberating on the establishment of a General Services Office to finally address the issues on PPE. If such is not possible, the Inventory Committee will be reorganized and recommendations on the one-time cleansing of PPE will be the priority of the Committee.
observations.reason: Not Reiterated in Part II

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor: a. to provide the Municipal Accountant the report of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2019 as basis for adjustment and to establish the certified list of taxpayers for CY 2020 tallied with the assessment report of the Municipal Assessor as of December 31, 2019; b. together with Municipal Treasurer, to take the necessary measures to update the Report on RPTAR and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively; c. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the understatement noted and for any amount of receivables from prior years based on the submitted report of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2019, set-up the RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2020 and adopt the Modified Accrual Method in accounting for real property taxes. Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enjoin all the concerned officials to strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: No action taken Implemented No action taken Implemented No action taken Implemented

observations.recommendation:
In view of the regulation on the one-time cleansing of PPE, we recommended and the
Management agreed to consider requiring the Municipal Treasurer/Designated GSO or
create and require an Inventory Committee to dedicate a period of the year, as guided
by a Physical Inventory Plan, on which complete and thorough physical count of the
PPEs will be conducted in accordance to the said Circular. After which, require the
immediate reconciliation of count results with the accounting records to facilitate the
review of the noted variances, effect the necessary adjustments, provision of necessary
disclosures in the notes to financial statements, and the necessary disposition for
non-existing/missing items.

To retrospectively apply the changes in revenue recognition, we recommended and the
Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to draw adjusting journal entries to
record as receivable the Municipal share from the 2017 to 2020 RPT and SET that are
not collected as of 2020 year-end and recognize income therefrom. Prospectively, we
also recommended and they agreed to set-up of receivables and income for the shares
of the Municipality in the RPT and SET every beginning of calendar year.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize income for
penalties only for the Municipal share and record them under Tax Revenue - Fines and
Penalties – Property Taxes account.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. record the capital expenditures of ₱18,270,602.69 as assets and increase
government equity thru prior period adjustments in the books of accounts;

b. recognize income in the Trust Fund books for any subsequent utilization of funds
received from national agencies or any other sources that are considered as non-
exchange transactions; and

c. prepare and submit for succeeding years the Statement of Financial Performance
for the Trust Fund along with the other financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to
provide the necessary disclosures on reconciliation in the Notes to Financial
Statements as required in IPSASs 2 and 24 and ensure its prompt submission to the
Commission on Audit.

Therefore, we recommended and the Management agreed that to require the Municipal
Treasurer to a) prepare and submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the Schedule of
Unreleased Commercial Checks in time for the closing of the books to enable the reversion
back to cash in bank and payable accounts of the amount of unreleased checks; and b)
for the checks which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the
existence of checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of new checks.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant of
the immediate submission to the Audit Team of the missing DVs and their related
supporting documents as well as the lacking supporting documents noted, otherwise,
transactions shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the
disbursement vouchers, their supporting documents and liquidation reports and
documents of the noted cash advances;

b. designate more special disbursing officers to avoid simultaneous cash advances by a
single accountable officer; and

c. require liquidation of the ₱362,000.00 cash advances for the cancelled training.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. MDRRO to submit copies of the work plan for the provision of relief goods to each
household and see to it that the date and items being distributed are indicated on the
distribution list;

b. Municipal Accountant to submit the original copy of sales invoice and collection
receipt issued by BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines as well as distribution
list for the relief goods, face mask, face shield and school supplies;

c. BAC to ensure that the name and address of the entity, printed name of the person
who provided the quote, contact number, email address (if any) are indicated in the
Request for Quotation and to submit the following:
- approved Annual Procurement Plan or any supplemental or revision thereof
inclusive of all purchases made in response to COVID-19 pandemic;
- BAC Resolution for the justification of alternative mode of procurement and
recommendation for the award of the contract, proof of posting of Request for
Quotation, Notice of Award and Purchase Order, ITR for CY 2019, original copy of
Omnibus Sworn Statement (OSS) and Mayor’s Permit or Business Permit for CY
2020 of BSMA Trading for the purchase of medicines; and
- justification for inconsistencies in the posting of Notice for Negotiated
Procurement in the PhilGEPs, Contract Agreement and distribution of the relief
goods purchased from L and H Marketing under Check No. 1758900.

Therefore, we recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to specify the details of purpose of hiring boats as transportation in order to
substantiate the propriety of each transaction.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor ensure the
optimal utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund and require the:

a. Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating expenditures that are not allowed
to be charged against 20 percent Development Fund and record obligation upon
entering into contract for project implementation;

b. Municipal Accountant to submit the sales invoice and collection receipt for the
purchase of 3 units aircondition from Kendra Aircondition Trading;

c. Former Acting MAO to provide the Audit Team with the detailed plan for the Greening
Project and its proper costing and justification on the noted discrepancies between
their reported distribution of seedlings with the result of confirmation with the 13
Punong Barangays, as well as the status of the trees planted;

d. BAC Chairperson provide copy of Invitation to Bid posted in the PhilGEPs and
provide explanation for the inconsistencies noted on the posted Award Notice
Abstract; and

e. MPDC and the Municipal Budget Officer to provide information on actual use/benefit
derived from the bulldozer since its major repair.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment for erroneous transfer of
unutilized capital outlay amounting to ₱150,000.00 and for the payments made to job
order workers totaling ₱27,115.00; to prepare Report on Sources and Utilization of
DRRMF and submit same to the Audit Team on or before 15th day after the end of
each month; and to provide disclosure on the amount and details of the unexpended
balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements;

b. Municipal Budget Officer to see to it that only disaster risk management activities will
be charged to the LDRRMF; and

c. Municipal Disaster Risks Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) provide the
Audit Team with supplemental investment plan for the utilization of unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years in the Special Trust Fund amounting to ₱1,568,050.00;
henceforth, ensure to include list of projects and activities that will be charged to the
unexpended LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate
caption.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Personnel
Officer to submit to the Audit Team copy of all authority to report to work issued during the
period of Enhanced Community Quarantine (March 17- May 15, 2020) and the
supporting documents such as Daily Time Record, and accomplishment reports,
attendance sheet/logbook for the regular and job order employees to avoid audit
suspension.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant of
the immediate submission of the missing documentary requirements. Otherwise, the
said transactions may be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant
should ensure the completeness of the documents authenticating the claims for
reimbursement, with specification of the particular purpose of travel, prior to its
approval and processing.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to justify the
necessity of the specific laptop model purchased, subject to evaluation. Otherwise, such
transaction may not be allowed in audit. We also recommend that the Municipal Bids
and Award Committee (BAC) justify in writing the excessive price availed for the unit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee headed
by the Municipal Treasurer update the PPE count conducted in 2016 and reconcile
results with the accounting records to establish the missing/unlocated, transfers or
unserviceable properties.

We also recommended and they agreed to undertake the immediate disposal of
unserviceable PPE to declutter the accounting books of non-value items.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer:
a. to provide the Municipal Accountant the report of delinquent taxpayers as of
December 31, 2019 as basis for adjustment and to establish the certified list of
taxpayers for CY 2020 tallied with the assessment report of the Municipal Assessor
as of December 31, 2019;
b. together with Municipal Accountant, to establish receivable amounts from every
taxpayers per assessment roll and maintain subsidiary ledger for each; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for the
understatement noted and for any amount of receivables from prior years based
on the submitted report of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2019, set-up
the RPT and SET Receivables for CY 2020 and adopt the Modified Accrual Method
in accounting for real property taxes.

Likewise, we also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enjoin all the
concerned officials to strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to
minimize tax delinquencies.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to properly account for the distribution of the proceeds of real
property tax in accordance with Section 271 of RA 7160; to determine the correct
balance of the Due to LGUs (Province and Barangays) in coordination with the
Municipal Treasurer and make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
affected accounts; and to strictly adhere with the modified accrual method of
accounting for the real property tax; and
b. Municipal Treasurer to make representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office
with regards to the erroneous remittances of professional tax and to make the
necessary adjustment for the next remittances.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately deposit the ₱206,401.67 balances as of March 12, 2020 under General
Fund and deposit intact and regularly all collections.

We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to observe the process of
realignment/supplemental for any augmentation or changes in budget allotments that
was neglected in ₱116,000.00 of the ₱166,000.00 budget deficit. We also
recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the refund of the remaining ₱50,000.00
disbursed without proper budget obligation.

Moving forward, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to require all
financial assistance granted by the Office of the Mayor be supported with a valid
identification of the payee and basis of crisis situation being claimed for.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer and Budget Officer monitor the cash collections for every
release of allotment from the approved budget before incurring obligations; and
b. Local School Board provide an augmentation thru supplemental budget for every
use of cash from savings of SEF; and
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record/adjust
accordingly the expense in SEF and General Fund and recognize the corresponding
intra receivable and payable arising from the purchase under SEF but paid from the
General Fund.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the refund of the payments made
totaling ₱31,500.00. We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to fill in the evident
need for additional personnel and consider hiring instead of procuring services of private
individuals.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer agreed to
compute and claim from the contractor the liquidated damages incurred for the three
(3) projects. Otherwise, such amount of damages not deducted from the payment to
the contractor shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant, as part of the BAC-TWG, agreed to
submit the noted lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit. Likewise, we
recommended that the BAC properly evaluate the qualifications of the bidder at any
stage of the procurement process.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Bids and Awards
Committee:
a) with the Municipal Engineer to immediately submit the noted lacking documents
for the procurement process and on the evaluation made for the grant of project
time extension, otherwise the payments shall be suspended in audit; and
b) to render a detailed report on post-qualification undertaken for every bidding.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to demand from the
contractor the refund of ₱265,620.28, otherwise, such overpayment shall be
disallowed in audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to cause the immediate
submission of the noted lacking documents. Otherwise, transactions shall be
suspended in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer, Treasurer
and Accountant to cause the refund of the total amount of ₱296,900.00. We also
recommended to stop hiring laborers and incorporate all necessary
construction/preparatory works in the Program of Works for projects awarded to
contractors.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
a. concerned contractors to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform with
the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance
Security/Warranty Security and consider blacklisting of the concerned contractors
who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support the
action taken; and
b. inspectorate Team to conduct regular and strict monitoring and supervision to ensure
that all contractors undertake the projects in accordance with the approved program
of works.

We recommended and the Management agreed to impose and deduct the appropriate
liquidated damages upon final payment to the contractor.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the:
• concerned contractors to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted to conform
with the approved program of works. Otherwise, forfeit the Performance

who refuses to effect the necessary corrections and submit documents to support
the action taken; and
• inspectorate Team to conduct regular and strict monitoring and supervision to
ensure that all contractors undertake the projects in accordance with the approved
program of works.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to provide the pre-repair inspection report and a written
and valid justification on the propriety and necessity of the repair, subject to
evaluation, otherwise the said transaction shall be disallowed in audit;
b. Municipal Engineer to demand from the contractor the detailed costing of the

and refund the resulting cost difference plus liquidated damages for the noted
delay; and
c. Municipal Engineer to submit for evaluation the approved plan for the
excavation itemizing the site, dimensions, scope of work, and others lacking
details noted and the Bids and Awards Committee for the invitation request to
other contractors, otherwise suspended in audit.

We also recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to
draw a correcting journal entry for the understated depreciation of the equipment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit the liquidation reports
and documents of ₱558,275.00 cash advances, documents showing the visited
places and activities undertaken for the educational tour, and official and
acknowledgement receipts for the accommodation and boat hire totalling
₱45,600.00. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
b. participants to the educational tour to refund the excess per diem claimed of ₱480
per person; and
c. Municipal Treasurer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to provide
explanation for inconsistencies noted for the expenditure of ₱49,750.00 for
Meeting with Tourism Stakeholders cum Year End Assessment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and the concerned officials/employees to submit the
liquidation reports and documents of ₱114,600.00 cash advances, Itinerary of
Travel and Reimbursement Expense Receipt (RER) for van hire totalling
₱121,743.10. Otherwise, said transactions shall be suspended in audit.
b. concerned officials/employees to refund the excess ₱95,700.00 travel expenses
and ₱4,900.00 registration fee; and
c. attendees of 2nd Local Legislation Enhancement Seminar (LLES-2) to refund the
expenses totalling ₱90,839,32 that could have been dispensed with if the seminar
attended was held in Luzon. Likewise, attend seminars, trainings and other
related activities within the geographical island and take full cognizance of their
principal responsibility and accountability relative to the prudent use of scarce
financial resources pursuant to DILG Memorandum No. 2011-59.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to immediately submit to the
Audit Team the missing DVs and their related supporting documents as well as the
lacking supporting documents noted, otherwise, transactions shall be suspended in
audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct all departments that in
the preparation of their Purchase Orders, detailed information are to be indicated
particularly, the specifications of the items being procured. Also, for the items/goods
procured under the listed PO’s without details, we recommended the submission of the
pictures of the items delivered in order to ascertain the type/kind of item procured.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
General Services Officer to include only those PPEs that are counted by the Inventory
Committee during the physical inventory taking in the RPCPPE and reconcile results
with the accounting records to establish the missing/unlocated, transfers or
unserviceable properties. Considering these findings on PPE as a perennial issue of
the Municipality, we recommended that these procedures be done by account until all
PPE accounts in the financial statements are completed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine those PPEs which had
no available documents and disclose to the Notes to the Financial Statements as well
as the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and Inventory Report.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Treasurer to perform her function as follows:

a. provide the Municipal Accountant the certified list of taxpayers with the
corresponding amount due and collectible for the CY 2019 and years thereafter
pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and

b. prepare the list of delinquent real property tax as of year-end before the schedule
of finalization of financial statements and provide the Municipal Accountant a copy
thereof.

Moreover, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record all real
property taxes in Modified Accrual Method of Accounting as soon as the amount of
collectibles per taxpayers is established.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed as required by the Municipal
Mayor to submit the disbursement vouchers and all supporting documents of the
recorded payables totaling ₱236,576.75 pursuant to Section 39 of PD 1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed as required by the Municipal
Mayor to immediately submit the missing DVs and its supporting documents to the
Auditor and strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations relative to the timely
submission of accounts to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

Otherwise, we recommended and the Management agreed to cause the suspension of
the payment of the salaries of the officials concerned upon failure to submit the aforesaid
DVs and its supporting documents pursuant to Section 122(2) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the Management agreed to stop allotting Engineering and
Administrative Overhead for the infrastructure projects costing ₱1,000,000.00 and
below pursuant to existing law and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to support accomplishment reports as proof of services rendered
by the laborers hired in the project monitoring charged against EAO and stop payments
without complete documentations. We also recommended and the Management agreed
to stop the practice of contracting services for functions that are among the mandates of
the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and instead consider hiring additional
manpower.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require all trainings be supported with proposals detailing the cost to be incurred,
as head of the Local School Board; and

b. prioritize the provision of basic education of the out-of-school children, youth and
adults in the locality and maximize the utilization of the very limited resources in
the Special Education Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) consider the readiness of the
Municipality in planning projects and procurement of equipment that are necessary to
address the disaster risk mitigation needs of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor and the
LDRRM Council clearly specify the detailed descriptions, locations and cost of
priority programs, projects and activities funded out of LDRRMF. For projects that
are presented in generic terms and with lump-sum appropriation, secure the approval
of the Sangguniang Bayan before the implementation and disbursements of the fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

a. re-evaluate the readiness of the Municipality for the use and maintenance of the
noted equipment and vehicles before pursuing the purchase; and

b. appropriate funds from the 20 percent Development Fund for projects that are
ready for implementation to ensure optimal utilization and the immediate delivery
of benefits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer to include only those PPEs that are counted by the Inventory Committee during the physical inventory taking in the RPCPPE and reconcile results with the accounting records to establish the missing/unlocated, transfers or unserviceable properties. Considering these findings on PPE as a perennial issue of the Municipality, we recommended that these procedures be done by account until all PPE accounts in the financial statements are completed.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant determine those PPEs which had no available documents and disclose to the Notes to the Financial Statements as well as the results of reconciliation of RPCPPE and Inventory Report.

observations.recommendation:

a. the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Assessment Roll and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively;

b. the Municipal Treasurer to prepare, maintain and furnish to the Municipal Accountant an updated duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year and prior years; and

c. the Municipal Accountant to record all real property taxes in Modified Accrual Method of Accounting as required under the Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, for a fair presentation of financial statements.

observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor updated the Assessment Roll and provided the Municipal Treasurer. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer still in the process of updating their records. Compliance of the Municipal Treasurer in the previous audit recommendations did not result in non-implementation of Modified Accrual Method of Accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare an Annual Investment Plan which shall clearly specify the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the Municipality to be implemented during the year and those that are to be implemented, in compliance with the above cited regulations.
observations.managementAction: There was no majority of its programs, projects and activities (PPAs) by object of expenditure and the Municipal programs, projects and activities (PPAs) intended for implementation were not included in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: There was no submission of all on-going projects/ programs/ activities (PPA) at the beginning of the year. The PPAs were not submitted timely to the Audit Team. The Municipal Engineer and other concerned officials to submit all on-going and to be implemented projects during the year in compliance with the cited regulations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare an Annual Investment Plan specifying the programs, projects and activities that will facilitate measurement of performance as against targeted results and to enhance the use of the AIP as a budgetary control tool.
observations.managementAction: Annual Investment Plan for CY 2019 was prepared specifying the programs and projects and activities and not by account of expenditure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the requirements of Treasury Circular 02-2009 on the bonding of public officers and ensure that the Local Chief Executive of the LGU is covered by fidelity bond. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer's Office strictly monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of the fidelity bonds of their Accountable Officers and see to it that renewal be made a month before its expiry. No Accountable Officer shall be allowed to continue performing their duties and responsibilities with expired bonds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and her three staff already renewed their bond which effective until October 5, 2019 and September 27, 2019, respectively. No Accountable Officer shall be allowed to continue performing their duties and responsibilities with expired bonds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. Municipal Accountant and the MSWDO to ensure that the payrolls are supported with properly accomplished payrolls which are duly signed by the authorized signatories and that the pay-out to the social pension recipients are supported with payrolls with signatures/fingerprint of the beneficiaries and authorized signatories;

b. OSCA and the MSWDO to conduct the necessary checking and verification/back ground check and home visitation in determining the qualified senior citizen. The final list of the Social Pensioners and any amendments thereto should be properly communicated, certified, and approved; and

c. the LGU Disbursing Officer with the assistance of the MSWDO to properly conduct the pay-out and payroll liquidation of the social pension to ensure correctness of the amount given to rightful beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Payrolls are now duly signed by the authorized signatories. The MSWDO, OSCA and MSWDO conducted necessary checking and verification and ground check and home visitation in determining the qualified senior citizen. The final list of the social pensioners were certified and communicated. Pay-out was conducted to the senior citizens listed and the payroll liquidation was submitted to COA.

There was no separate Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC). There was no establishment of the solid waste management board as prescribed. The Municipality designated the Sanitation Inspector as the Solid Waste Management Focal Person per memorandum Circular No. 3 issued by the Municipal Mayor dated July 2, 2018. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Plan was subject for revision. There were verbal agreements with NGOs and junkshops for MRF. MSWMB convened and the Sanitation Inspector was designated as the focal person.
observations.reason: Verification on the status of the Solid Waste Management Plan learned that the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan was subject for revision. MSWMB still did not convene to discuss and make actions on this matter. The noted lacking documents were not fully prepared. No full establishment of the separate Local Council for Children.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. establish the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC). Thereafter, direct the members of the LCPC to prepare a Local Investment Plan for Children and Annual Investment Plan for Children; and

b. instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the LGU’s budget and maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC Fund to identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the LCPC.
observations.managementAction: There was no LCPC. The Municipal Mayor issued a memorandum and assigned the Sanitation Inspector as the Municipal Solid Waste Management Focal person. The PPAs for children were incorporated in the approved Investment Plan and Annual Budget.
observations.reason: There was no separate Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC). The PPAs for children were incorporated in the approved Investment Plan and Annual Budget but LCPC was not yet established. No full compliance with Section 15 of RA 9344.
