observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
1. the Designated General Services Officer to:
a) ensure that corresponding Acknowledgment Receipt for Equipment
(ARE) are issued for Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)
acquisitions of the municipality;
b) religiously perform the actual physical count of PPE by asking
assistance from all department heads and accountable personnel in
the conduct of complete and actual inventory count of the properties
on their accountability and generate the Report on the Physical Count
of PPE with complete data and following the prescribed format in Sec.
57 of NGAS Manual on LGUs, Volume II; and
c) verify the existence of reported unserviceable properties through
inspection to come up with a correct report.
2. the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded assets
arising from Trust Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. The Local Finance Committee to immediately adopt appropriate control
measures to eliminate cash overdraft pursuant to Section 337 of RA 7160;
b. Local Finance Committee to adopt strategy in order to increase
revenue/collections and monitor properly the availability of cash every
month to ensure that all income due the Municipality be collected in full and
assure continuous flow of funds and that the trust funds received be utilized
only for the specific purpose for which it was created;
c. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare Daily Cash Position Report to serve
as guide to him in certifying that funds are available to ensure that all
disbursements have cash back up;
d. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the
disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the
total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of
cash overdraft; and
e. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to explain why
the Trust Fund was utilized and disbursed other than its intended purpose
contrary to Section 309 (b) of RA 7160 and Section 84 of PD No. 1445,
thus, resulted in the incurrence of cash overdraft amounting to ₱152,728.76
in the Trust Fund.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary
measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real
Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card,
respectively;
b. the Municipal Treasurer prepare, maintain and furnish to the Municipal
Accountant an updated duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers
and the amount due and collectible during the year and prior years; and
c. the Municipal Accountant record all real property taxes in Modified
Accrual Method of Accounting as required under the Section 20 of NGAS
Manual for LGUs, Volume I, for a fair presentation of financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to
reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by
identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers
for adjustment, if warranted. Likewise, analyze the Due to and Due from Other
Funds so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts.
Further, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made to ensure the
accuracy of the recorded financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the disbursement vouchers including the
supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate post audit to avoid
suspension and/or disallowance of the same. We also recommended the strict
compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-006 and 95-006 and
PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to submit immediately the lacking liquidation reports including the
supporting documents to the Audit Team to facilitate post audit to avoid
suspension and/or disallowance of the same. We also recommended the strict
compliance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 95-006.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the concerned
accountable officers submit the lacking documents for the settlement of cash
advance relative to their attendance to the 2017 PCL Provincial Congress at
Davao City and refund the unnecessary expense of ₱2,880.00 for seat selection
fee.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer and other concerned officials to submit at the beginning of the year the
lists of all on-going government projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) and those
that are to be implemented during the year, in compliance with the above cited
regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Budget Officer to
prepare an Annual Investment Plan (AIP) which shall clearly specify the
programs, projects and activities (PPAs) of the Municipality to facilitate
measurement of performance as against targeted results and to enhance the use
of the AIP as a budgetary control tool.

We recommended that the management strictly observe the requirements
of Treasury Circular 02-2009 on the bonding of public officers and ensure that
the Local Chief Executive of the Municipality is covered by fidelity bond.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer’s Office strictly
monitor the effectivity and expiry dates of the fidelity bonds of their Accountable
Officers and see to it that renewal be made a month before its expiry. No
Accountable Officer shall be allowed to continue performing their duties and
responsibilities with expired bonds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant and the MSWD Officer to ensure that the
liquidation reports for payment of Social Pension are supported with
properly accomplished payrolls which are also duly signed by the
recipients and all authorized signatories;
• OSCA and the MSWDO to conduct the necessary
verification/background check and home visitation in determining the
final list of the Social Pensioners and any amendments thereto should be
properly communicated, certified, and approved; and
• LGU Disbursing Officer with the assistance of the MSWDO to properly
conduct the pay-out and liquidation of the social pension to ensure
correctness of the amount given to rightful beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• review the composition of the Solid Waste Management Board and, if
necessary, re-organize it in accordance with the requirements provided
under Section 12 of RA No. 9003 to ensure effective and efficient
formulation and implementation of the Municipality’s Solid Waste
Management Plan;
• designate a head of the Office of the MENRO on a concurrent basis to
ensure that various solid wastes concerns are immediately
managed/resolved;
• instruct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) to
compile the documents pertaining to the Solid Waste Management Plan
(SWMP) of the Municipality, verify the status of its approval with the
National Solid Waste Management Commission, and regularly convene to
enable effective performance of its functions;
• encourage and support the establishment of Multi-Purpose Environment
Cooperatives or Association to help promote the implementation of RA
No. 9003; and
• thru the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWMB) require
strict implementation of all Municipal Ordinances and impose penalties
to those who will be caught violating the same.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• establish the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).
Thereafter, direct the members of the LCPC to prepare a Local
Development Plan for Children and Local Investment Plan for Children;
and
• instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the LGU’s Share
from IRA for LCPC and maximize utilization of the allocated LCPC
Fund to identified PPAs provided for in DILG MC No. 2012-120 dated
July 4, 2012 to ensure full strengthening and implementation of the
LCPC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to make the correcting entries to adjust the noted
erroneous recording of transactions;
b. Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documentation and the
disbursement voucher not found in audit to avoid suspension; and
c. Municipal Budget Officer to refund the ₱250.00 excess disbursement over
appropriation for certifying for the existence of available appropriation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the assigned
department head/s or personnel to fast track the submission of the Agency
Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) of the CY 2016 Annual
Audit Report, and every year thereafter to the Auditor concerned to facilitate
monitoring and validations of the actions taken by the LGU on the related audit
observations and recommendations. Compliance on the submission of the same
report to the above-mentioned oversight bodies should also be made.

We recommended that the Local Development Committee optimize the
utilization of 20% Development Fund through: (a) proper planning of project
including timing of execution; (b) coordinated action from LGU officials for
better result; and (c) strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure
accomplishment of the project so that the desired services and benefits can be
delivered to the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and
Awards Committee, Members of the Technical Working Group and all the
concerned officials to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 on their procurement process and immediately submit the
lacking documents for further evaluation in compliance to COA Circular No.
2012-001 to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension and/or Disallowances.
Moreso, to submit photocopies of valid IDs of the laborers/payees and
accomplishment reports relative to the disbursements made charged against this
fund.

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee submit
justification/explanation why the projects were awarded to the prospective
bidder in spite of absence of various relevant eligibility documents.

In view of this deficiency, we strictly recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure completeness of supporting documents relative to the
disbursement of funds to validate the propriety of claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting entries to arrive at
the corrected balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM and ensure that
proper recording of similar transactions will be adopted in the future;
b. The MDRRMO to implement the Capital Outlay projects as
appropriated and indicated in the LDRRMF Investment Plan;
c. The Municipal Accountant to strictly adopt regulations on liquidation of
cash advances particularly in ensuring the completeness of the supporting
documents; and
d. The Municipal Accountant and concerned personnel submit the lacking
disbursement vouchers to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipality thru its GAD Focal Point System
plan and implement projects and programs that will directly benefit women.
Consider the potentials and borne skills of women in the formulation of
strategies and bases that will be used in designing priority projects and
programs for GAD Plan responsive to its prescribed thrust and vision.

We recommended also that the GAD Focal Point Team strictly monitor
the implementation of the planned GAD program, projects and activities to
ensure that the plan was fully implemented. With these, the intent of GAD on
promoting gender-responsive governance, protecting and fulfilling women’s
human rights, and promoting women’s empowerment as mandated could be
fully achieved.
We recommended that the MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the concerned Auditor for audit.
observations.reason: No reason was provided by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: the Sangguniang Bayan establish specific guidelines in granting AICS for medical, burial, educational and other financial assistance to the indigent residents that is aligned/patterned with the DSWD issued guidelines; the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and concerned officials stop the practice of payments of financial assistance on reimbursement basis and ensure that subsequent payments be made directly to the beneficiaries or through cash advance of the designated disbursing officer; the Municipal Treasurer ensures that donations for barangay activities are made payable in the name of the Barangay itself and not on the personal account of any barangay official. Require recipient barangays of the financial assistance to issue official receipts to acknowledge receipt of cash assistance and facilitate proper accounting; the Municipal Mayor, OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of giving financial assistance directly in the name of the PNP officials and the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents and ensures that subsequent payments were supported with all the required documentary requirements.
observations.managementAction: The lacking documents were not yet submitted. Prior to the knowledge of audit observation, the municipality had already processed donations in the personal account of the Punong Barangays of Tingloy. Prior to the knowledge of audit observation, the municipality had already disbursed financial assistance directly in the name of the PNP officials. The OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents and ensures that subsequent payments were supported with all the required documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Specific guidelines for AICS are yet to be established. There are indigent people who have no IDs to present in the bank to withdraw the assistance if paid through check directly named to indigent therefore the assistance were given through reimbursement basis. No reason was provided by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete and actual physical count of all LGU’s properties, prepare an inventory report thereon complying with the format required by NGAS Manual on LGUs and submit the same to the Audit Team. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the unrecorded assets arising from Trust Fund. Further, we recommended that the Management review the charging of expenses made particularly those which were recorded as expense but should have been capitalized being directly attributable to a PPE item and effect appropriate adjustments to arrive at the correct balance of PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and effect proper reclassification of entries and be cautious in recording every transaction in the municipality’s books.
The Municipal Accountant/Municipal Treasurer remit intact all taxes withheld from payment to the Bureau of Internal Revenue on the 10th day of the month following the transactions.
the Municipal Accountant start maintaining separate subsidiary ledger for every project of a source agency and trace the details of the presented balances to come up with the correct ending balance per program/project.
the concerned personnel coordinate with the source agency itself regarding the return of the remaining balances of the completed programs/projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

the Municipal Assessor and the Municipal Treasurer take the necessary measures to update the Report on Real Property Assessment and the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card, respectively.
the Municipal Treasurer prepare, maintain and furnish to the Municipal Accountant an updated duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year and prior years.
the Municipal Accountant record all real property taxes in Modified Accrual Method of Accounting as required under the Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, for a fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to continuously coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to reconcile the balances of the reciprocal accounts Due from/Due to LGUs by identifying the causes of discrepancies in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers for adjustment, if warranted.  Likewise, analyze the Due to and Due from Other Funds so that appropriate adjustments can be made on the affected accounts.  Further, periodic reconciliation of the accounts should be made to ensure the accuracy of the recorded financial transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to transfer the agency’s unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRM-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned personnel to provide written explanation to the audit team on the noted observations as to the purchase made. Likewise, instruct the BAC Committee to strictly comply with the regulations on procurement through alternative mode. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant to check on the accuracy of the supporting documents prior to processing of payments and be cautious in using accounts for recording transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit all the lacking documents relative to the implementation of the project and payments made and ensure the completeness of all supporting documents before processing payments. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer properly monitor and review the reported charges against appropriations and ascertain the accuracy of the details reflected in the Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligation and other reports.

We reiterated our recommendation that the MDRRMO prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the concerned Auditor for audit.

We recommended that:

the members of the BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit justification why the project was awarded to the winning contractor in spite non-submission of the documentary requirements of RA 9184. Request the submission of the lacking documents in order to further validate the propriety of the disbursements.
the Municipal Mayor request the contractor to complete and rectify the unfinished project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties, if warranted, or the Head of the Procuring Entity, upon verifying the existence of such ground, cause the execution of a Verified Report with all relevant evidence attached and convey the termination of the contract through a written notice to the contractor.
the Municipal Engineer should intensify the monitoring of all on-going projects to avoid delays incompliance with the approved contract period.
the Municipal Accountant to draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) taking up the unrecorded properties to state correctly the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment and Government Equity accounts as of December 31, 2016.
the concerned management officials to submit copy of contracts within five (5) days upon execution as prescribed under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to provide the Auditor sufficient time to evaluate those documents and inform the management of deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the required reports and to strictly adhere with the rules on disclosing information in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. The members of the BAC and the Technical Working Group to submit justification why the project was awarded to the winning supplier/contractor in spite non-submission of the documentary requirements of RA 9184. Request the submission of the lacking documents in order to further validate the propriety of the disbursements.
b. the concerned management officials to submit copy of contracts within five (5) days upon execution as prescribed under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to provide the Auditor sufficient time to evaluate those documents and inform the management of deficiencies, if any.
c. the BAC and TWG members of the Municipality to ensure that all the procedures and documents necessary in the conduct of public bidding and in the implementation of the project are strictly complied with. If needed, the BAC members and all the Municipal Officials involve in procurement goods and infrastructure project of the Municipality should revisit and understand thoroughly the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR for proper conduct of public bidding up to the implementation of the contract.
Otherwise, any loss or damage the Municipality might incur due to the failure of the members of the BAC and Municipal Official who approved the transactions to properly observe and implement the provisions of the above law would be their personal liabilities and that sanctions provided under the law may even be imposed to the said officials for non- compliance thereto.
d. the Municipal Accountant to process and pay only claims with complete documentation pursuant to the above auditing regulation.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should carefully review the documents supporting the claims and prove the accuracy and validity of payment, otherwise, cause the refund of the discrepancy noted on payment of labor payrolls.
e. the Municipal Official concerned fill-up the dates in the disbursement voucher, purchase request, purchase order, inspection and acceptance report, etc.  and ensure complete information/data on official receipt to facilitate the immediate verification of documents and to determine as to when such supporting documents was properly executed in order not to cast doubt as to the integrity and authenticity of the document submitted to the audit team.   Likewise, the end-user/requesting official should also specify the complete specifications of the items being requisitioned in the Purchase Request as a guide of the procurement officer/bids and awards committee in the purchase of the items.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor refrain/stop the practice of payments under reimbursement basis and strictly comply with the established rules and regulations on payment of wages for the laborers, which is through cash advances by the disbursing officer of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the members of the BAC, Secretariat, and the Technical Working Group to immediately submit all the lacking documents noted therein to further evaluate the disbursement together with the justification on why the procurement was made in spite absence of the required documents and/or non-compliance with the mandated provisions of the Government Procurement Law.
b. the BAC of the Municipality to ensure that all of the eligibility documents be submitted within the prescribed period and conduct eligibility checking and screening in accordance with the procurement law.
c. the concerned Municipal Officials submit copy of contracts and its supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five (5) days from perfection pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:

strictly comply with the prescribed procedures and guidelines under RA No. 9003 in implementing solid waste management programs
compile the documents pertaining to the Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality and verify the status of its approval with the National Solid Waste Management Commission
enact ordinances necessary enforcing compliance particularly to waste segregation and proper disposal of residual waste and imposing appropriate penalties for non-cooperation applicable to all barangays of Tingloy
require the barangays to re-establish and effectively maintain their materials recovery facility that will receive the mixed waste for final sorting, segregation, composting or recycling

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor discontinue the practice of releasing Special Education Fund as financial assistance to school personnel; instead, observe the provisions of RA 7160 and related Joint Circulars in the utilization of the fund.

Likewise, we recommended that the LSB/Municipal officials be always guided by the provisions of RA 9184 and it’s Implementing Rules and Regulations when making procurement and ensures the completeness of supporting documents prior processing of payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other concerned officials and employees strictly observe direct payments of transactions to the suppliers/creditors, otherwise, payments can be made in the form of cash advance by the disbursing officer for cases provided by COA Circular 97-002.

Likewise, for the reimbursed fuel/gasoline expenses, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, officials and employees strictly comply with the requirements on the use of government vehicles which include instituting appropriate controls and measures on the purchase and issuance of gasoline.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that cash advances are only granted to proper accountable officers. Likewise, ascertain that only the disbursing officer performs disbursing functions.
b. the Municipal Accountant to limit the use of cash advances to those mentioned in COA Circular No. 97-002 unless it is impractical to do so
c. the Municipal Accountant religiously practice proper verification and checking of supporting attachments to liquidations, acknowledgment of liquidation by signing and indicating the date of receipt of report to validate the liquidation made by the accountable officer.
d. the Municipal Accountant/concerned accountable officers/employees submit the lacking documents as required and remedial course of actions on discrepancies noted.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 April 22, 2016.  Contributions and elective officials’ expenses related to leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality.  Likewise, cause the refund of ₱2,500.00 discrepancy in payment to Aces, Inc.

We recommended that all municipal officials and employees primarily accountable for government property prepared a complete record of all properties under their custody and submit the same to the designated GSO pursuant to Section 375(d) of RA 7160 to facilitate the complete physical count of all property, plant and equipment.  We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee that will conduct thorough review of the Report of Physical Count of PPE through actual count, inspection, verification and validation of all properties of the Municipality.  Likewise, the designated General Services Officer coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Accountant and perform the reconciliation of all PPE accounts in order to establish the causes of discrepancies, whether items had already been missing, donated, sold or unserviceable in order to arrive at the correct balance of PPE accounts.  Further, the OIC-Municipal Accountant and designated GSO maintain a complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Acting General Services Officer conducted physical inventory taking of Office Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures, IT Equipment, Motor Vehicles, Water Crafts and Other PPE accounts but only limited to those with records on hand.
observations.reason:
Lack of manpower

Lack of personnel- The OIC-Municipal Accountant has one staff only and the Acting GSO is also the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor prepare and maintain an assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties and submit to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to Sections 205(a) and 248 of RA 7160.  We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare, maintain and furnish to the OIC-Municipal Accountant an updated duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year and prior years.  Likewise, the OIC-Municipal Accountant record all real property taxes in Modified Accrual Method of Accounting as required under Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, for a fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Assessor prepared an assessment roll and forwarded the data to the Municipal Treasurer.  The Municipal Mayor discussed the issue with the Department Heads.
observations.reason: The Assessment Roll was not yet updated due to lack of manpower and the Acting Municipal Assessor who was the former Assistant Municipal Assessor has no staff yet.  The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of maintaining an updated duly certified list of taxpayers.  The certified list of taxpayers was not provided to the OIC-Municipal Accountant resulting to non-recognition of RPT and SET Receivables in the books.  Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: the Acting HRMO and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that all terminal leave benefits due to retired officials and employees of the Municipality have a corresponding appropriation and obligate the same to record the unpaid balances in the books for a fair presentation of liability accounts; the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Mayor prioritize the payments of terminal leave benefits; the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents and ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations. Refrain from processing and approving payments with incomplete documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The said terminal leave benefits have been budgeted in CY 2017.  Management made partial payment to some claimants.  Payment of terminal leave benefits for CY 2016 were supported with the required documents.  The Municipal Accountant-OIC submit the lacking documents and ensure that all claims against government funds are supported with all the required documentations. Refrain from processing and approving payments with incomplete documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: There are still claimants of unpaid terminal leave benefits.  No reason was provided by management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” (Code 438) in the Trust Fund books for a fair presentation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant transferred the unexpended balance to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liability-DRRM” (Code 438) in the Trust Fund Books for a fair presentation of financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: the OIC-Municipal Accountant ensures the completeness of all supporting documents before processing for payment and submit all the lacking documents relative to the implementation of the project and payments made to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit; the Municipal Budget Officer verify and check the submitted Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligation and ensures the accuracy of the charges to the appropriation and allotment; the Municipal Mayor, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer ensures the accuracy of information in Invitation to Bid posted at PhilGEPs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of all supporting documents before processing for payment and submit all the lacking documents relative to the implementation of the project and payments made. There are still lacking documents in similar transactions of the Municipality. Refer to finding no. 5.c

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Sangguniang Bayan Members, Municipal Budget Officer and Local Finance Committee strictly comply with the general limitations on personal services pursuant to Section 325(a) of RA 7160 and considered the findings on the budget review conducted by the Provincial Finance Committee. Moreover, refrain from providing additional PS expenditures until such time that the PS cap has been observed. Require the municipal officials and employees to refund the proportionate amount received in granting the Fourth Tranche Salary Schedule in CY 2015.

No monetization of leave credits was processed/disbursed in CY 2016. The Acting HRMO reflect the leave credit balance in the approved Application for Leave that will support the claims of the Municipal officials and employees in the monetization of their leave credits.
observations.managementAction: No monetization of leave credits was processed/disbursed in CY 2016.  Nobody claimed monetization of leave credits for CY 2016.  Refund has been made by the Municipal Mayor and SB Member as per OR nos. cited.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit their legal basis on granting Representation Allowance and Transportation Allowance totaling ₱73,500.00. Non submission thereof may result in disallowance in audit. Henceforth, ensure compliance on granting of such allowances in accordance with Local Budget Circular No. 103.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant stopped granting Representation Allowance and Transportation Allowance to the designated MDRRMO. Ms. Ceferina Mendoza is making refund on disallowed RATA amounting to ₱21,000.00 (ND No. 2016-001 (2015) while Ms. Evangeline Garcia with disallowed RATA for ₱52,500.00 (ND No. 2016-003 (2015) neither appealed nor refunded the said amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the practice of payments under reimbursement basis and strictly comply with the established rules and regulations on payments of wages for the laborers, which is thru cash advances by the disbursing officer of the Municipality. Further, the Municipal Mayor and the Foreman refund the amount of ₱100,000.00 and ₱9,000.00, respectively. observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: the BAC Members, Secretariat, TWG and Municipal Engineer ensure that all of the bidding documents were prepared following the format prescribed by the GPPB and make it available to all prospective bidders pursuant to RA 9184, its IRR and GPPB issued guidelines; the BAC conduct pre-procurement conference pursuant to Section 20 of RA 9184; Advertisement and publication in the newspaper of general circulation were done on projects with an Approved Budget for the Contract of ₱5 million and above and post the copy of Invitation to Bid in the conspicuous place in the Municipality premises pursuant to Section 21 of RA 9184 and its Revised IRR and ensures the accuracy and consistencies on the information indicated therein; the BAC conduct pre-bid conference in accordance with Section 22.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; the BAC ensure that all of the eligibility documents be submitted within the prescribed period and conduct eligibility checking and screening in accordance with the procurement law; the concerned Municipal Officials submit the contract and its supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five days from perfection pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001; the Municipal Mayor strictly comply with the proper procedures in processing legal documents and ensures accuracy of every provisions indicated therein; the Municipal Engineer inspect all of the infrastructure projects implemented by the Municipality and prepare report thereon; the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents before processing for payment to avoid suspension and disallowances in audit; the Municipal Accountant submit the computations and bases on the payments made; the Municipal Budget Officer submit written explanation/justification why the appropriations for BUB project was obligated in other projects; the Municipal Mayor ensures the strict implementation of contract agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor implement strictly provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority. Sign appropriate issuances to institutionalize the establishment of GAD Focal Point System in the Municipality. We also recommended that the Designated Chairman-GFPS prepare and submit the GAD Plan and Budget within the prescribed period and secure the approval of DILG Regional Director to ensure that the plan programs, projects and activities were all gender responsive and to efficiently and effectively address the gender issues within its jurisdiction. Further, the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator with the assistance of GFPS maintain master list per sectoral group for PWD and Senior Citizen.
